intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling seedling/stem. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but the Pothos is no longer visible or has suffered total senescence.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos absent, only anchor remains)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: No evidence of Mexican Mint; pot appears to be part of the shared substrate with P4.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos missing; white rabbit anchor present.
plant_audit.P4: Single, thin, etiolated stem/sprout present. Likely a new germination or remnant.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking drainage.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4/P2 pot, acting as a calcium supplement or pH buffer intervention.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: Severe light starvation; transition to blue-spectrum-heavy night mode renders biological assessment difficult but confirms lack of photosynthetic activity.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green leaf in P3 and a thin sprout in P4.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Progressive loss of visible foliage. By T-1 and Current, the environment has shifted to low-light/night-mode, obscuring detail but confirming the absence of robust plant life.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Critical/Terminal. The biome is failing. The lack of foliage suggests either total senescence or extreme etiolation leading to death. The environment is currently incapable of supporting the registered species.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in the soil; total disappearance of the Pothos specimen.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic decline in the health of the indoor desk biome. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens have either perished or been removed. The current state is characterized by bare soil and a single, weak sprout in the P4/P2 container. The environment is severely light-deprived, and the lack of structural integrity in the remaining plant matter suggests a failure in basic horticultural maintenance.
